message:
fix(select): poll both futures each tick so loser gets initialized

Previously Select short-circuited on the left future: if A was
immediately Ready, B was never polled at all. This meant any
initialization side-effects in B (registering wakers, starting I/O,
allocating resources) were skipped entirely.

Now both futures are polled unconditionally each tick, with results
captured into Option locals. Left-bias is preserved: if both are
Ready, the left (A) value wins. But the right future always gets
at least one poll opportunity for initialization.

Updated test assertion: the loser is now polled exactly once for
initialization (previously expected 0 polls when the left-biased
winner was immediately ready). The loser is still dropped without
being drained to completion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
